author: Roughness
text:
§10 blocker model: predicates, front-door reciprocals, gating plugin

Core gains the §10 read-only predicates Task::ready() (unclaimed with every claim-blocker resolved — the Status::Ready rung) and Task::closeable() (every close-blocker resolved, checked at close.pre); both are best-effort hints over an injected resolver, enforcing nothing.

The create front-door now authors reciprocal edges: Create.parent carries (id, On), so --parent writes a {child, claim} blocker on the epic and --gates writes {child, close} on the parent; --needs is a claim-blocker on the child. Task-file IO is lifted to a shared taskfile module (read/write/task_ids plus exists, the §10 file-existence resolver, and the idempotent add_blocker).

Adds the default gating plugin as its own gate binary — the SOLE enforcer: at claim.pre/close.pre it reads the §7 payload and rejects (exit 1) when any blocker gating the op names a still-existing task file. Core stores blockers and enforces nothing; this tiny plugin does.
